content: In core agent API, AgentPromptOptions was removed. prompt() now takes (userMessage, signal?) only. reservePercent remains in CompactionConfig, sessionId is stored on Agent and passed via LoopRuntime (setSessionId), and debugThreadId/debugTurnId flow was removed from SessionManager/thread handlers.
Config와 Options를 명확히 구분: Config는 설정값 전용, Options는 함수의 optional 인자 전용. signal 같은 런타임 제어 인자는 Config에 넣지 않는다.
사용자는 한국어로 대화하며, 요청 시 최대한 깊고 신중한 설명을 선호한다.

ProviderManager now uses injected ExternalProviderAuth bindings for provider-specific auth, and ChatGPT OAuth is bound from runtime via createChatGPTOAuthBinding instead of being managed directly inside core ProviderManager.

TUI inline renderer must cap/redraw the active viewport by physical terminal row count, not logical rendered line count. Wrapped active lines otherwise spill into scrollback and can appear as repeated/duplicated sentences during ongoing redraws or user scrollback interaction.
Agent 생성 옵션에서 LLM 스트림 주입 키는 streamFn이 아니라 llmMsgStreamFn이다. 테스트/팩토리에서 streamFn을 넘기면 provider resolver fallback으로 흘러가며 fake/test provider에서 즉시 실패한다.
TUI provider key 저장 경로에서 RPC client가 있으면 로컬 파일(saveAuthKey) 대신 app-server AUTH_SET 요청을 우선 사용해 providerManager 런타임 상태를 즉시 동기화한다. 그렇지 않을 때만 saveAuthKey fallback을 사용한다.

TUI /model picker now shows models only from authenticated providers (providerManager.hasKeyFor). If none are authenticated, it falls back to showing only the current provider's models so picker remains usable.
Backlog tracking moved from .claude skill to add_knowledge category: removed .claude/skills/backlog and added "backlog" to KnowledgeType/add_knowledge schemas across runtime/protocol/debug-viewer.

Test file convention standardized: place tests only under each package's test/ directory (no src/**/__tests__), mirror src path inside test, use *.test.ts(x) for unit and *.integration.test.ts(x) for integration, keep shared utilities in test/helpers and fixtures in test/fixtures, and keep E2E tests under packages/e2e.
Replaced add_knowledge with update_knowledge tool in runtime. New tool supports action-based upsert/delete and KnowledgeType now excludes decision, using backlog as category for backlog management.

Knowledge RPC was simplified: removed knowledge/add and knowledge/delete methods, and unified write operations under knowledge/update with action: upsert|delete while keeping knowledge/list for reads.

TUI renderer packages/cli/src/tui/render-blocks.ts handles summary/key_value/list/table/tree/status_badges only. Protocol ToolRenderBlock additionally includes file/command/diff, and runtime deriveToolRenderPayload can emit file/command/diff for read/write/bash/edit/multi_edit/apply_patch. In current TUI these blocks fall into default and are skipped, so chat-view header appears but structured detail lines are not rendered.
User prefers fewer round-trips: batch grep/glob/read operations where possible instead of issuing one tool call at a time repeatedly.

TUI Thinking body uses MarkdownView (chat-view addThinkingMessage), so markdown strong tokens in reasoning are rendered as ANSI bold via markdown.ts renderer.strong. The t.boldOff prefix in thinking line rendering only prevents style bleed from header; it does not disable bold inside markdown content.
In TUI chat-view, Thinking detail blocks are rendered as plain text lines (split by newline) rather than MarkdownView, so raw markdown markers like ** and ` remain visible in reasoning output.

TUI renderer bug root cause for disappearing past output: when active region exceeded terminal rows, sliceLinesToTerminalRows dropped prefix for viewport display but renderer still erased/redrew only active region, so clipped prefix was never persisted to scrollback and could appear lost after redraw. Fix pattern: promote clipped active prefix to committed flush (except while overlay is visible) and clamp committedCount by allLines.length before slicing.

packages/cli/src/tui/framework/__tests__/renderer.test.ts has a persistent failing case 'cursor stays anchored when active content ends on wrap boundary' in current branch; this failure reproduces even when only running renderer suite and is unrelated to render-block fallback hardening.

ThreadStatus now includes "compacting" in addition to "idle" and "busy". Use "busy" for turn execution and "compacting" specifically for thread compaction, including thread/status/changed notifications and frontend busy checks.

Thread status should remain idle|busy only; compaction UI is signaled via compaction_start/compaction_end events by changing spinner/status text (e.g., to Compacting…) rather than introducing a separate compacting thread status. This must also handle mid-turn compaction by restoring the previous active status text after compaction_end while thread stays busy.
For OAuth module naming in core auth, prefer chatgpt-specific naming (e.g., auth/chatgpt-oauth) rather than openai-oauth, since the flow is specifically ChatGPT OAuth in this project context.

Using console.debug inside runtime render-payload derivation emits multiline logs that interleave with TUI spinner/status redraw, producing visually garbled mixed lines. The logs still show correct payload conversion metrics (e.g., changedCount/sampleBefore/sampleAfter).

In packages/cli/src/tui/markdown.ts applyGfmPostProcessing, footnote-definition detection must use stripAnsi(line) before regex matching, because paragraph rendering may already wrap lines with ANSI color codes; matching raw line misses [^id]: definitions and skips footnote section rendering.

The old e2e comment claiming turn initiator does not receive userMessage item notifications is stale. Current runtime routing delivers item/started userMessage to subscribed/current-thread connections including initiator (verified in packages/e2e/turn-execution.test.ts run with AppServer emit-debug logs).

In CLI TUI steering flow, on agent event type 'steering_injected' the app should always drain runtime pendingSteers and set viewModel pending steers to [] regardless of text matching, then backfill committed user messages from event payload text when needed.

packages/runtime/src/session/manager.ts is a major coupling point: it currently combines session lifecycle/resume, append-only persistence, agent run orchestration, compaction handling, pending/staged entry visibility, steering queue management, and event enrichment. Direct runtime dependents include app-server/thread-handlers.ts, app-server/server.ts, and collab/registry.ts, so safer refactors should preserve the SessionManager public surface while extracting internals behind helper classes/services first.

In this project, pending steering follow-up is client-driven. The server should not auto-submit a new turn from pending steering on turn end/interruption; CLI decides whether to resubmit or discard queued steering.
